channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABPZ20 KNHC 231719
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
1100 AM PDT Thu Jul 23 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Hurricane
Fausto, located well west-southwest of the southern tip of the Baja
California Peninsula.

Eastern and Central East Pacific (EP98):
A broad area of low pressure located several hundred miles south
of the coast of southern Mexico is producing a large area of
showers and thunderstorms. Environmental conditions appear
conducive for gradual development of this system, and a tropical
depression is likely to form during the next couple of days, while
it moves generally west-northwestward at 10 to 15 mph, remaining
well offshore of Mexico.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...high...70 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...high...90 percent.

Well Southwest of Hawaii:
An area of low pressure located well southwest of the Hawaiian
Islands is producing disorganized showers and thunderstorms.
Environmental conditions appear conducive for some gradual
development of this system while it moves slowly west to
west-northwestward, and a tropical depression is likely to form
after it enters the western Pacific basin over the weekend or early
next week.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...20 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...high...70 percent.

&&
Products issued by the U.S. Joint Typhoon Warning Center can be
found at: www.metoc.navy.mil/jtwc/jtwc.html

Official tropical cyclone forecast information issued by the Japan
Meteorological Agency for the western Pacific basin can be found at:
www.jma.go.jp/jma/jma-eng/jma-center/rsmc-hp-pub-eg/RSMC_HP.htm

$$
Forecaster Roberts
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Hurricane Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...FAUSTO IS SLIGHTLY STRONGER... As of 11:00 AM HST Thu Jul 23 the center of Fausto was located near 18.2, -126.9 with movement W at 14 mph. The minimum central pressure was 980 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 85 mph.

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Public Advisory Number 20
item.description:
Issued at 1100 AM HST Thu Jul 23 2026 
000
WTPZ31 KNHC 232037
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Hurricane Fausto Advisory Number  20
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 AM HST Thu Jul 23 2026
 
...FAUSTO IS SLIGHTLY STRONGER...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 1100 AM HST...2100 UTC...INFORMATION
-----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...18.2N 126.9W
ABOUT 1145 MI...1845 KM WSW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
ABOUT 1840 MI...2965 KM E OF HILO HAWAII
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...85 MPH...140 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...W OR 280 DEGREES AT 14 MPH...22 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...980 MB...28.94 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 1100 AM HST (2100 UTC), the center of Hurricane Fausto was 
located near latitude 18.2 North, longitude 126.9 West. Fausto is 
moving toward the west near 14 mph (22 km/h), and this general 
motion is expected to continue over the next few days.
 
Maximum sustained winds have increased to near 85 mph (140 km/h) 
with higher gusts. Small intensity fluctuations are possible today, 
followed by slow weakening through the weekend.
 
Hurricane-force winds extend outward up to 45 miles (75 km) from the
center and tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 185 miles
(295 km).
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 980 mb (28.94 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF: Swells generated by Fausto are affecting the west coast of the
Baja California Peninsula and the coast of southern California.
These swells are likely to cause life-threatening surf and rip
current conditions. Please consult products from your local weather
office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_ep1.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 500 PM HST.
 
$$
Forecaster Reinhart
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 20
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
855 
WTPZ21 KNHC 232036
TCMEP1
 
HURRICANE FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  20
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
2100 UTC THU JUL 23 2026
 
HURRICANE CENTER LOCATED NEAR 18.2N 126.9W AT 23/2100Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST OR 280 DEGREES AT  12 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  980 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  75 KT WITH GUSTS TO  90 KT.
64 KT....... 40NE   0SE   0SW  30NW.
50 KT....... 80NE  40SE  40SW  70NW.
34 KT.......150NE 160SE  90SW 150NW.
4 M SEAS....270NE 150SE 150SW 270NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 18.2N 126.9W AT 23/2100Z
AT 23/1800Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 18.1N 126.2W
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0600Z 18.5N 128.9W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 30NE   0SE   0SW  20NW.
50 KT... 70NE  40SE  30SW  50NW.
34 KT...150NE 140SE  60SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1800Z 18.8N 131.7W
MAX WIND  70 KT...GUSTS  85 KT.
64 KT... 30NE   0SE   0SW  20NW.
50 KT... 60NE  40SE  30SW  50NW.
34 KT...150NE 140SE  70SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/0600Z 19.2N 134.3W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 20NE   0SE   0SW  10NW.
50 KT... 60NE  40SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 120SE  70SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/1800Z 19.5N 136.5W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 20NE   0SE   0SW  10NW.
50 KT... 60NE  40SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT...130NE 110SE  70SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 26/0600Z 19.8N 138.8W
MAX WIND  60 KT...GUSTS  75 KT.
50 KT... 50NE  30SE  20SW  40NW.
34 KT...120NE 100SE  60SW 100NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 26/1800Z 20.1N 141.3W
MAX WIND  55 KT...GUSTS  65 KT.
50 KT... 40NE  30SE  20SW  40NW.
34 KT...110NE  90SE  50SW  90NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 27/1800Z 20.7N 147.0W
MAX WIND  50 KT...GUSTS  60 KT.
50 KT... 40NE  20SE   0SW  30NW.
34 KT... 90NE  60SE  30SW  80NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 28/1800Z 21.2N 153.1W
MAX WIND  45 KT...GUSTS  55 KT.
34 KT... 80NE  60SE  40SW  70NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 18.2N 126.9W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 24/0300Z
 
$$
FORECASTER REINHART
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 20
item.description:
Issued at 1100 AM HST Thu Jul 23 2026 
000
WTPZ41 KNHC 232038
TCDEP1
 
Hurricane Fausto Discussion Number  20
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 AM HST Thu Jul 23 2026
 
The satellite presentation of Fausto has improved during the past 
several hours. The hurricane has a ragged eye, with a ring of cold 
cloud tops surrounding the center associated with deep convection. 
Based on these structural improvements, the initial intensity is 
raised to 75 kt, which falls between recent D-PRINT (72 kt), D-MINT 
(76 kt), and Dvorak (77 kt) intensity estimates. 

A well-established ridge to the north of Fausto continues to steer 
the hurricane westward (280/12 kt). This ridge will remain the 
dominant steering feature during the next several days, keeping 
Fausto on a westward heading as it crosses into the Central Pacific 
basin. The track model envelope has tightened up this cycle, with 
decreased cross-track spread compared to earlier today. Since the 
bulk of the guidance trended slightly northward, the updated NHC 
track forecast is nudged in that direction to reflect the latest 
model consensus aids (HCCA and TVCN). While the longer-range 
forecast brings the center of Fausto generally toward the main 
Hawaiian Islands, it is too early to discuss any potential impacts.

After struggling with dry air intrusions earlier today, Fausto has 
managed to close off an eyewall. Slight additional near-term 
strengthening cannot be completely ruled out if the hurricane 
maintains this structure. However, water vapor imagery indicates the 
hurricane is moving into an increasingly drier air mass over 
marginal (26-26.5C) SSTs, and there is the potential for westerly 
mid-level shear to introduce drier air into the circulation once 
again. The longer-term NHC forecast shows gradual weakening through 
the weekend and into early next week, but still remains on the 
higher end of the intensity guidance. This forecast keeps Fausto a 
tropical cyclone through day 5, but the drier and more stable air 
mass over the Central Pacific will likely make it difficult for 
Fausto to maintain organized convection by early next week.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  23/2100Z 18.2N 126.9W   75 KT  85 MPH
 12H  24/0600Z 18.5N 128.9W   75 KT  85 MPH
 24H  24/1800Z 18.8N 131.7W   70 KT  80 MPH
 36H  25/0600Z 19.2N 134.3W   65 KT  75 MPH
 48H  25/1800Z 19.5N 136.5W   65 KT  75 MPH
 60H  26/0600Z 19.8N 138.8W   60 KT  70 MPH
 72H  26/1800Z 20.1N 141.3W   55 KT  65 MPH
 96H  27/1800Z 20.7N 147.0W   50 KT  60 MPH
120H  28/1800Z 21.2N 153.1W   45 KT  50 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Reinhart
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 20
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 232037
PWSEP1
                                                                    
HURRICANE FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  20                
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
2100 UTC THU JUL 23 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 2100Z THE CENTER OF HURRICANE FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR LATITUDE   
18.2 NORTH...LONGITUDE 126.9 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS NEAR 
75 KTS...85 MPH...140 KM/H.                                         
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        18Z THU AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       18Z THU 06Z FRI 18Z FRI 06Z SAT 18Z SAT 18Z SUN 18Z MON
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             06Z FRI 18Z FRI 06Z SAT 18Z SAT 18Z SUN 18Z MON 18Z TUE
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
20N 125W       34  9   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)
 
20N 130W       34 26  53(79)   X(79)   X(79)   X(79)   X(79)   X(79)
20N 130W       50  X   6( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)
 
20N 135W       34  X   1( 1)  54(55)  27(82)   1(83)   X(83)   X(83)
20N 135W       50  X   X( X)  16(16)  25(41)   1(42)   X(42)   X(42)
20N 135W       64  X   X( X)   3( 3)  10(13)   X(13)   X(13)   X(13)
 
20N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  65(66)   2(68)   X(68)
20N 140W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  29(29)   1(30)   X(30)
20N 140W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   9( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)
 
20N 145W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  34(35)   1(36)
20N 145W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   9( 9)   1(10)
20N 145W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)   X( 3)
 
20N 150W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)  16(19)
20N 150W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)
 
20N 151W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  16(17)
20N 151W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)
 
20N 154W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  10(10)
 
HILO           34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)
 
21N 156W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)
 
KAILUA-KONA    34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
KAHULUI        34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)
 
HANA           34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)
 
LANAI CITY     34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
KAUNAKAKAI     34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER REINHART
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Graphics
